player_utterance: You've been quiet a long time, John. I want to know what you actually believe about what happened here.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, clicking a funeral home pen against his thumb

player_utterance: I was at the service. I heard the eulogy too. I'm not from the congregation.

believer_state_after.posture: standing, pen now still in his hand, looking at the player more directly

player_utterance: I came because I knew the family. And I wanted to see who would show up.

believer_state_after.posture: standing, shifting his weight, the pen now in his pocket

player_utterance: I knew people he hurt. And I wanted to see if anyone else did too.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward on the bench, hands loose between his knees, looking at the player not at the ground

player_utterance: You knew, didn't you. Not all of it, maybe. But enough that you're sitting here like this.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head turned away slightly, looking at the candle

voice_offers.read: He's listening now; name what we both know.
voice_offers.line: You knew, didn't you. Not all of it, maybe. But enough that you're sitting here like this.

voice_offers.read: He leaned in; let him be the one to speak next.
Test what 'knew' means to a man who teaches history.
voice_offers.line: You teach how ordinary people lived next to things. What did you tell yourself you were seeing, with him?

player_utterance: Say his name. Say what you knew. You owe that much to the room.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting upright now, the funeral program flat on his knee, looking at the player directly

voice_offers.read: He didn't deny it. Press the silence into a name.
voice_offers.line: Say his name. Say what you knew. You owe that much to the room.

voice_offers.read: He has a vocabulary for this exact evasion. Use it.
voice_offers.line: You teach bystanders. What's the word you'd use for yourself, if he were in your lecture?

voice_offers.read: He turned toward the candle, not away from me.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer fast. I can wait with you.

player_utterance: His name, John. You've said grace over his casserole. Say it now with what you knew underneath.

believer_state_after.posture: standing now, the funeral program crumpled slightly in his hand, looking past the player toward the candle

voice_offers.read: He's upright, looking back; press while he's listening.
voice_offers.line: His name, John. You've said grace over his casserole. Say it now with what you knew underneath.

voice_offers.read: He met my eyes; that's not nothing, don't trample it.
voice_offers.line: I see you looking at me. I'm not trying to wreck you. I just need one true thing.

voice_offers.read: Make him name the category he'd assign anyone else.
voice_offers.line: In your classroom, what do you call the neighbor who sees and keeps teaching? Give me the term.

player_utterance: The candle isn't going to say it for you. Open your mouth.

believer_state_after.posture: standing very still, the program crumpled again in his fist at his side, eyes closed for a moment

voice_offers.read: Three blows landed in silence; one more and the name comes.
voice_offers.line: The candle isn't going to say it for you. Open your mouth.

voice_offers.read: He's standing, crumpling paper, not fleeing — give him room to arrive.
voice_offers.line: You're still here. That's something. I'll stop pushing for one breath.

voice_offers.read: He retreats into quotation; corner him inside his own discipline.
voice_offers.line: You quote when you can't speak plain. What's the primary source, John? Your own eyes saw what?

player_utterance: One word, John. Just the name. Then I'll stop.

believer_state_after.posture: standing still, the crumpled program loose in one hand at his side, looking at the player levelly

voice_offers.read: He's closed his eyes; the next push might break something unmendable.
Three blows landed; one more and the name falls out.
voice_offers.line: One word, John. Just the name. Then I'll stop.

player_utterance: The name, John. You promised yourself you'd say it someday. Today is someday.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands empty in his lap, looking at his palms

voice_offers.read: He's a breath from it; one more nudge finishes.
voice_offers.line: The name, John. You promised yourself you'd say it someday. Today is someday.

voice_offers.read: He is breaking in front of me; pressure now is cruelty.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to perform it for me. Just say it to the floor if you need to.

player_utterance: Say it. Whoever he was, he deserves his name in your mouth.

believer_state_after.posture: standing up from the bench slowly, looking toward the house, then back at the player

voice_offers.read: He's almost there; one more nudge cracks it open.
voice_offers.line: Say it. Whoever he was, he deserves his name in your mouth.

voice_offers.read: His hands are empty; meet them, don't strike them.
voice_offers.line: Whoever he is — I'll sit here while you find him. No more orders.

player_utterance: Don't walk away from it now. You're already halfway through the word.

believer_state_after.posture: turned mostly toward the house now, one hand on the doorframe of the courtyard gate

voice_offers.read: He's rising; one more push and the name falls out.
voice_offers.line: Don't walk away from it now. You're already halfway through the word.

voice_offers.read: He's standing, looking home; the blade has cornered a grieving man.
voice_offers.line: Sit back down, John. The name can wait. I shouldn't have cornered you like that.

voice_offers.read: He looked at the house — someone there knows the name too.
voice_offers.line: Who's inside, John? Who else carried this with you?
